content: 2024–2025 state-of-the-art active learning agent memory systems can be restructured around a nested "agent-of-agents" memory architecture, where the core active learning selector itself is a lightweight curiosity-driven meta-agent that curates which existing agent-specific episodic memories get consolidated into long-term storage for future active learning queries. This meta-agent leverages the intrinsic reward signals from ICM/RND curiosity frameworks to prioritize unincorporated, surprising memory traces that are most likely to reduce future generalization error, rather than prioritizing memory by recency or task frequency.
